subparts.text: Explain the significance of standard deviation in real-life data analysis. 1
Calculate Mean, Median, and Mode for the following dataset: 3, 7, 5, 9, 7, 5, 6 1
List the assumptions of a simple linear regression model. 2
In what situations is Spearman’s rank correlation preferred over Pearson’s? 2
Explain the additive and multiplicative models of time series analysis. 3
Discuss the difficulties in constructing index numbers. 3
Define conditional probability with an example. 4
Why the normal distribution is considered important in statistics? 4 CO Mapping
Explain the four levels of measurement—nominal, ordinal, interval, and ratio—with appropriate examples.
Discuss the properties, merits, and demerits of Mean, Median, and Mode. When should each be used? 1
For the following data, calculate Range, Quartile Deviation and Mean Deviation: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70
Find the Standard Deviation and Coefficient of Variation for: 14, 18, 20, 22, 24, 26, 30 1
Define regression analysis. How does it differ from correlation? What are its major applications?
What is the standard error of estimate? How is it used in assessing regression model accuracy? 2
From the data: X: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 Y: 3, 5, 7, 9, 11 Find the regression equation of Y on X. Using the regression equation predict the value of Y when X = 12.
If regression coefficient of Y on X is 0.8 and X on Y is 0.6, find the value of r and interpret it. 2
Explain the method of least squares for measuring trends in time series data.
Differentiate between weighted and unweighted index numbers. Why are weights necessary? 3 2

questions.question_text: Fit a straight-line trend to the following data using the least squares method: Year 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020 Sales 200 220 250 270 300 3

subparts.text: Explain the Binomial distribution—its assumptions, characteristics, and applications.
What is the Central Limit Theorem? Explain its significance in sampling and statistical inference. 4
A bag contains 6 red balls, 4 green balls, and 5 blue balls. One ball is drawn at random. What is the probability that the ball is: red green or blue not red?

A company has two plants. Plant A produces 60% of the total output; defect rate = 2% Plant B produces 40% of the output; defect rate = 5%. If a product is found to be defective, what is the probability that it came from Plant B? 4
